--community.description: SNMP community of the server (SNMP v1/2 only)

--community2.description: SNMP community which can be used to switch the context during runtime

--contextengineid.description: The context engine id for SNMPv3 (10 to 64 hex characters)

--contextname.description: The context name for SNMPv3 (empty represents the default context)

--domain.description: The transport domain to use (default: udp/ipv4, other possible values: udp6, udp/ipv6, tcp, tcp4, tcp/ipv4, tcp6, tcp/ipv6)

--hostname.description: Hostname or IP-address of the switch or router

--lookback.description: The amount of time you want to look back when calculating average rates. Use it for mode interface-errors or interface-usage. Without --lookback the time between two runs of check_printer_health is the base for calculations. If you want your checkresult to be based for example on the past hour, use --lookback 3600.

--mitigation.description: The parameter allows you to change a critical error to a warning.

--mode.description: Which mode should be executed. Available modes: hardware-health, supplies-status and uptime.

--morphperfdata.description: The parameter allows you to change performance data labels. It's a perl regexp and a substitution. --morphperfdata '(.*)ISATAP(.*)'='$1patasi$2'

--name2.description: The secondary name of a component

--name3.description: The teritary name of a component

--negate.description: The parameter allows you to map exit levels, such as warning=critical

--offline.description: The maximum number of seconds since the last update of cache file before it is considered too old

--oids.description: A list of oids which are downloaded and written to a cache file. Use it together with --mode oidcache

--privpassword.description: The password for authPriv security level

--protocol.description: The SNMP protocol to use (default: 2c, other possibilities: 1,3)

--regexp.description: A flag indicating that --name is a regular expression

--report.description: Can be used to shorten the output.

--selectedperfdata.description: The parameter allows you to limit the list of performance data. It's a perl regexp. Only matching perfdata show up in the output.

--servertype.description: The type of the network device: cisco (default). Use it if auto-detection is not possible

--statefilesdir.description: An alternate directory where the plugin can save files

--units.description: One of %, B, KB, MB, GB, Bit, KBi, MBi, GBi. (used for e.g. mode interface-usage)

--username.description: The securityName for the USM security model (SNMPv3 only)

--with-mymodules-dyn-dir.description: A directory where own extensions can be found
